mission.title: kgateway: Client Load Balancing in ExtAuth server
mission.description: Client Load Balancing in ExtAuth server. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current kgateway deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kgateway version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway
helm list -n kgateway 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kgateway installation.

steps.title: Review kgateway configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kgateway configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway
kubectl get configmap -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kgateway
```
### Version

1.10.x (latest stable)

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

Currently if you configure passthrough auth with 10x replicas, the server chooses one and attaches to it, so the solution does not scale well.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Client Load Balancing in ExtAuth server
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kgateway
kubectl get events -n kgateway --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Client Load Balancing in ExtAuth server" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
A possible solution suggested by  
As outlined [here](https://github.com/grpc/proposal/blob/master/A9-server-side-conn-mgt.md) configuring _MAX_CONNECTION_AGE_ will close the connection after some time and allow the client to re-resolve the destination.

I've tested it with my little [server](https://github.com/solo-io/gloo/files/9432109/src.zip) and a low value (1 second).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kgateway installed or the issue environment reproducible.
